name: uphy-upgen
documentation:
# U-Phy Device Generator

U-Phy device generator.

## Developing

Python Poetry is recommended. See
https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installation for installation
instructions.

    $ cd /path/to/upgen
    $ poetry install
    $ poetry shell

## Build distributable binary using Nuitka

Install dependencies on Ubuntu::

    apt install patchelf python3-dev build-essential

The Nuitka compiler can be used to build a distributable binary. Note
that it should be possible to run the binary on the currently used and
all later Linux GLIBC versions, but not versions earlier than the one
in the build platform.

    $ poetry run nuitka \
        --include-package-data=upgen \
        --onefile \
        upgen/cli.py
    $ mkdir -p bin
    $ mv cli.bin bin/upgen

classes.name: upgen.model.uphy.Root

methods.name: upgen.model.uphy.Root.get_module_index
documentation: Get index in modules list from module id

The U-Phy data model is used to define fieldbus devices. A
device contains one or more slots. Each slot contains one
module. A module is a reusable component that can be instantiated
in more than one slot.

documentation: Use timestamp if available. If not, use current date

methods.name: upgen.generators.ethernetip.EipParam.reset
documentation: Reset class variables. Needed for automatic testing.

methods.name: upgen.generators.ethernetip.EipParam.create_params
documentation:
Prefered method to create parameters.
To handle array signals a list of parameters is returned.
An array signal with length n generates n parameters. For scalar signals the length of the list will be 1.

methods.name: upgen.generators.ethernetip.EipAssembly.reset
documentation: Reset class variables. Needed for automatic testing.

methods.name: upgen.generators.ethernetip.EipAssembly.remove_unused_assemblies
documentation:
Remove assembly objects with size == 0 from the Assembly objects list
This should only apply for input, output or config assemblies in the case
of a device does not have inputs, outputs or configuration parameters
Note that assemblies are only removes from the list, which is used to
generate the AssemN objects in [Assembly] section.
The assembly ids are still kept to create unique connection paths and
to avoid special cases in the corresponding enip adapter code.

methods.name: upgen.generators.ethernetip.EipAssembly.__init__
documentation:
Create assembly object. If a list of signal is passed as argument, then parameters
are created for all signals in the list and the parameters are appended to the assembly.
The param_prefix is used to create unique parameter names.

methods.name: upgen.generators.ethernetip.EthernetipGenerator.create_connections
documentation:
Generate connection objects from the device properties according to
the table below:

Device type         Exclusive Owner     Listen Only     Input Only
------------------------------------------------------------------
inputs + outputs    Yes                 Yes             Yes
inputs only         No                  Yes             Yes
outputs only        Yes                 No              No

methods.name: upgen.generators.profinet.ProfinetGenerator.export_file
documentation: Generate GSDML file by applying configuration in a U-Phy model to a template file

classes.name: upgen.generators.profinet.GsdmlGenerator

methods.name: upgen.generators.profinet.GsdmlGenerator.cleanup
documentation: Remove unused TextId from original template

Generator for converting a U-Phy model to a gsdml file.

The generation relies on a template that defines most characteristics.
GSDML template and and U-Phy device model in dict / json format.
Limitations:
- Only one device in model is supported
- Only characteristics defined by model updated, other characteristics needs to be changed in template, for example
  for a device with other port characteristics.
- TBD - extend model to cover everything?

documentation: Factory function for strings, as the length depends on the string
